stories.headline: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.summary: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.headline: Russia’s Lavrov visits China as US pressures Iran with Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: China and Russia have been vocal critics of the US-Israel war on Iran, an ally of both world powers.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.headline: Trump refuses to apologise after clash with Pope Leo XIV over Iran war
stories.summary: Despite widespread criticism including Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni's rare rebuke after the US president lambasted the US-born pontiff for being "weak on crime," Trump said no to an apology to the Catholic Church's Holy Father while reiterating his claims.

stories.summary: Ukrainian leader hopes for ‘pragmatic’ and ‘friendly’ relations with new government in contrast with hostility of previous pro-Russian regimein BerlinAt his press conference with Zelenskyy, Germany’s chancellor Friedrich Merz also welcomed Péter Magyar’s decisive victory, saying it would have “implications for our support for Ukraine”.“More Hungarians than ever before cast their votes. stories.headline: Ships test US naval blockade of Iran
stories.summary: US military says it will intercept any vessel leaving Iranian ports

stories.headline: How much will US Hormuz blockade hurt Iran, and does Tehran have an escape?
stories.summary: Iran earned $5bn in oil exports in past month while it shut the strait for most other ships. That revenue is at risk.

stories.headline: Chernobyl could face 'catastrophic' collapse as repairs stall following Russian drone strike
stories.summary: Failure to repair the protective structure around the nuclear site could unleash 'highly radioactive dust' that 'does not recognise borders', experts warn.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
stories.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

stories.headline: Trump Attack on Pope and Posting of Jesus Image Draw Widespread Criticism
stories.summary: A prominent conservative bishop was among the Catholic leaders who jumped to the pope’s defense.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy pitches new joint security system to European allies
stories.summary: Ukraine should be an integral part of Europe’s security, or some on the continent would be part of the "Russian world," Zelenskyy said as he announced discussions over a new joint defence system with EU and non-EU countries.

stories.headline: Trump deletes AI image depicting himself like Jesus after outrage
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has deleted an AI-generated image of himself depicted as a Jesus-like figure.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: JPMorgan and Citi post bumper profits as Iran war boosts traders
stories.summary: Jamie Dimon warns US economy faces ‘complex set of risks’ even as Wall Street banks benefit from market volatility

stories.headline: Lagarde Says EU Economy Between ECB Baseline, Adverse Scenarios
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde says the euro-zone economy has moved away from the central bank's Iran war base case, though not enough to currently warrant leaning toward raising interest rates. She speaks with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the IMF’s spring meetings. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank’s Saravelos Says Sell Dollar as War Risks Peaked
stories.summary: The risks linked to the war in Iran will be subsiding and it is time to bet on a weaker US dollar, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

stories.headline: Lagarde Says Europe’s Economy Has Slipped Below ECB Baseline
stories.summary: President Christine Lagarde said the euro-zone economy has moved away from the European Central Bank’s Iran war base case, though not enough to currently warrant leaning toward raising interest rates.

stories.headline: Cocoa Price Crash Spurs Hopes for Demand Recovery, Cheaper Candy
stories.summary: The collapse in cocoa futures is stoking optimism among some industry players that the pace of processing will improve as cheaper beans filter through the supply chain, easing the price of candy bars.

stories.headline: Market ‘Sugar High’ Will Send Stocks to Record, Wells Fargo Says
stories.summary: The war in Iran may be ongoing, but traders have largely moved on, as a number of bullish catalysts in the coming months are poised to propel the S&P 500 Index to new highs, according to Wells Fargo Securities.

stories.headline: Stocks Climb on Cooler Wholesale Price Growth, Lower Oil Prices
stories.summary: US stocks extended a rally Tuesday morning on a combination of cooler-than-expected US wholesale price gains and retreating oil prices, amid optimism that talks between the US and Iran to end the war may resume.

stories.headline: Breaking Down Earnings From JPMorgan, Citi and Wells Fargo
stories.summary: Big banks are sending mixed signals: trading desks are booming, dealmaking is back, and private credit risks look contained for now. But beneath the strong earnings, CEOs like Jamie Dimon of JPMorgan are still warning about an uncertain economy. Bloomberg's Katherine Doherty breaks down the earnings that came out this morning from JPMorgan, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.headline: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
stories.summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

stories.headline: Italy suspends defence agreement with Israel
stories.summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's government freezes automatic renewal of defence cooperation deal with Israel.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Futures Erase Gains on Oil Price Drop, Forecast
stories.summary: US natural gas futures fell after earlier gains as traders weighed plunging oil prices against mixed weather outlooks. Traders may also have more readily unwound long positions as forecasts later in April show weeks of mild weather despite a slight cold shift. Analysts also expect a growing storage surplus relative to the five-year average.

stories.headline: Lucid Names CEO as Saudi PIF, Uber Commit Another $750 Million
stories.summary: Lucid Group Inc. named a new chief executive officer and announced $750 million of fresh investment from its two biggest backers as the electric-car maker looks to shore up operations at a crucial juncture.

stories.headline: I Was ‘Madame Due Process,’ Ex-OPEC Head Says At Bribery Trial
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke said she sought to increase transparency and competition in Nigeria’s oil industry while she was minister overseeing the sector at a London trial where she faces criminal charges of bribery.

stories.headline: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
stories.summary: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.

stories.headline: Champions League: Barcelona handball claim against Atletico Madrid rejected
stories.summary: Barca were unhappy with an alleged handball by Atletico's Marc Pubill in quarterfinal first leg but UEFA rejects appeal.

Oldest gorilla in captivity celebrates 69th birthday in Berlin Zoo
stories.summary: Arriving in Berlin in 1959, the western lowland gorilla has far outlived the typical 35-40 year wild lifespan through specialised care.

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.summary: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.headline: Oil Drops as US and Iran Weigh More Talks With Blockade in Place
stories.summary: Oil slid on signs Washington and Tehran may revive talks following the start of a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, with traders monitoring vessels to see whether shipping traffic through the vital chokepoint will resume.

stories.headline: What the U.S. Blockade of Iran and the Strait of Hormuz Might Look Like
stories.summary: The U.S. military has provided few details on how it might carry out President Trump’s orders as he seeks to pressure Tehran on a peace deal. But history and established practices offer some clues.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
stories.summary: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.

stories.headline: Prosecutors Investigate ICE Agents Who Detained ChongLy Scott Thao in Minnesota
stories.summary: ChongLy Scott Thao, a naturalized U.S. citizen, was briefly detained during a federal immigration crackdown in Minnesota in January.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran

stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?
